jokes.text: Did he dye his mustache?
jokes.setup: Press briefing about the Israeli Prime Minister
jokes.punchline: Immediate focus on the superficial detail of his dyed mustache
jokes.explanation: Sharp observational comedy that deflates serious political moment with vanity focus

jokes.text: It's vanity. He's the TV face of the administration.
jokes.punchline: Ironic description of him as 'TV face' when he looks ridiculous
jokes.explanation: Sharp ironic observation about image vs reality in politics

jokes.text: But he's glued Cheetos to that face.
jokes.setup: Discussion of his appearance as TV face
jokes.punchline: Absurd visual description of mustache as glued Cheetos
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd visual metaphor that's both specific and hilarious

jokes.text: He looks like Yosemite Sam.
jokes.punchline: Comparison to cartoon character with prominent mustache
jokes.explanation: Solid visual comparison joke, well-executed but fairly predictable

jokes.text: Yeah, without the credibility.
jokes.punchline: Deadpan observation that cartoon character has more credibility
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan topper that elevates the previous joke with surgical precision

jokes.text: He's gonna get his tits done next.
jokes.punchline: Absurd escalation to breast enhancement
jokes.explanation: Good escalation but relies on shock value more than clever construction

jokes.text: She feels great, unlike President Harrison who contracted pneumonia during his inaugural address and died.
jokes.setup: How she feels about the milestone
jokes.punchline: Morbid comparison to dead president
jokes.explanation: Dark humor but feels a bit forced and over-explained

jokes.text: Nice work, Mike. You're on fire. Nope, wait. It's your mustache.
jokes.punchline: Literal interpretation referring to his orange mustache
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection that brings back the mustache running gag

jokes.text: I call it Tangerine Dream.
jokes.punchline: Pretentious name for his ridiculous facial hair
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Mike's oblivious self-regard

jokes.text: Hey, are we getting cocktails later? No, I can't. I've got to get home. You... Can you imagine? No. We love to drink.
jokes.punchline: Awkward revelation that someone has home obligations
jokes.explanation: Awkward social interaction but lacks punch

jokes.text: So you want me to rule the House rules committee.
jokes.setup: Discussion about influencing the rules committee
jokes.punchline: Pun on 'rule' as both influence and govern
jokes.explanation: Mild wordplay that's more clever than funny

jokes.text: Yeah, if anyone can do this, Dan, I'm confident you or the guy we hired to replace you can.
jokes.punchline: Immediate undermining by mentioning his replacement
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection that builds up then devastates in one sentence

jokes.text: Middle East, middle easy.
jokes.punchline: Childish wordplay showing obliviousness to complexity
jokes.explanation: Groan-worthy pun that reveals character's simple-mindedness

jokes.text: Whatever. I'm handling world peace, okay? You just go make the room pretty, i.e., the opposite of that fucking monstrosity.
jokes.punchline: Brutal insult about the current artwork
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing priorities and casual cruelty

jokes.text: What is that? Picasso's heavy period?
jokes.punchline: Play on 'Blue Period' vs 'heavy period'
jokes.explanation: Clever art reference with crude double meaning

jokes.text: President Hughes loved it. Is that why his wife tried to kill herself?
jokes.punchline: Dark suggestion that bad taste drove spouse to suicide attempt
jokes.explanation: Shockingly dark callback that's both horrifying and hilarious

jokes.text: I think it's called 'A Man Who Shits Triangles.'
jokes.punchline: Crude literal interpretation of abstract shapes
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly crude title that deflates artistic pretension

jokes.text: I just got a text from Amy. 'I'm a latte'? Huh? Guess she means she's late.
jokes.punchline: Autocorrect confusion between 'late' and 'latte'
jokes.explanation: Standard autocorrect joke that's relatable but predictable

jokes.text: Could be anything... accident up ahead, active shooter. Well, probably not that.
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions mass shooting then backtracks
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about modern anxieties with awkward backpedal

jokes.text: You make me any more late, and I lock you in this car and have it compacted.
jokes.punchline: Absurdly violent threat involving car compacting
jokes.explanation: Perfectly Amy-like threat that's specific and terrifying

jokes.text: Memo to self... it is winnable. Delete the private polling. Also, hair. Ask Marianne about electrolysis.
jokes.punchline: Personal vanity notes mixed with campaign strategy
jokes.explanation: Good character moment revealing Selina's priorities and insecurities

jokes.text: No offense, honey, but, I mean, look at yourself.
jokes.punchline: Mother's brutal assessment of daughter's appearance
jokes.explanation: Perfectly horrible mother-daughter dynamic captured in one line

jokes.text: She turned around the college film society. I transformed it, Mom.
jokes.punchline: Catherine's overblown description of minor achievement
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Catherine's desperate need for recognition

jokes.text: I feel like I'm on trial in the future.
jokes.punchline: Dystopian interpretation of modern technology
jokes.explanation: Great sci-fi reference that captures the alienation of video calls

jokes.text: Amy, it's filtering out like every other word. Usually I just do that myself.
jokes.setup: Bad video connection cutting out words
jokes.punchline: Selina admits she usually ignores Amy anyway
jokes.explanation: Perfect Selina moment - turning technical problem into personal insult

jokes.text: Have you seen that thing? It's like he ate a volcano.
jokes.punchline: Volcanic metaphor for orange facial hair
jokes.explanation: Brilliant visual metaphor that's both specific and universally funny

jokes.text: We polled her popularity. How dare you? How could you have the audacity to... Oh. Oh, these are not good.
jokes.punchline: Immediate deflation when seeing the terrible numbers
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection showing Selina's shallow principles

jokes.text: I outlived some dead idiot president. Wow.
jokes.setup: Being told about outlasting William Henry Harrison
jokes.punchline: Dismissive summary of historical milestone
jokes.explanation: Perfect Selina dismissal that deflates any sense of achievement

jokes.text: Happy birthday, Ben. Wow, that was quick. Yeah. We have a cake? No.
jokes.punchline: Perfunctory well-wishes followed by no preparation
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing workplace dysfunction

jokes.text: I could go get you some Pringles or...
jokes.setup: Trying to make Teddy feel good
jokes.punchline: Pathetically small gesture offered
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jonah moment showing his limited understanding of favors

jokes.text: You simultaneously made my day and ruined his.
jokes.setup: Being told about opportunity to work with Dan
jokes.punchline: Recognition of zero-sum office dynamics
jokes.explanation: Smart observation about office politics delivered with Jonah's naive honesty

jokes.text: Did we get these from a homeless man's grave? They're molesting my eyes.
jokes.punchline: Absurd origin story and violent metaphor
jokes.explanation: Strong visual description with good escalation to 'eye molestation'

jokes.text: I heard you sent them back to centerpiece hell.
jokes.punchline: Creating specific hell for bad decorations
jokes.explanation: Nice world-building joke creating hierarchy of decoration quality

jokes.text: Eliminate weaknesses. That's a little Nazi doctor.
jokes.punchline: Amy's comparison to Nazi medical experiments
jokes.explanation: Sharp, dark comparison that cuts through corporate-speak

jokes.text: Well, I just wanted to say a friendly hello in an unfriendly way.
jokes.punchline: Perfectly contradictory statement of intent
jokes.explanation: Perfect Amy line - precise, hostile, and self-aware

jokes.text: I mean, who knew we even had one?
jokes.setup: Learning about Native American artist in White House
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of ignorance about diversity
jokes.explanation: Good satire of performative diversity and institutional ignorance

jokes.text: To be honest, I don't even know if she's one of ours. 20 bucks says she's Mossad.
jokes.punchline: Casual paranoia about Israeli intelligence
jokes.explanation: Good paranoid humor that fits the political setting

jokes.text: This is about the brand image of Catherine Meyer, TM.
jokes.punchline: Treating daughter like a trademarked product
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kent moment - reducing human relationships to business terms

jokes.text: Catherine, America doesn't like you.
jokes.setup: Building up to delivering bad news
jokes.punchline: Brutally direct statement to president's daughter
jokes.explanation: Devastatingly direct delivery that's both shocking and perfectly Kent

jokes.text: I wouldn't say hate. You just... polarize opinion with the bulk of it gravitating to this pole here.
jokes.punchline: Technical euphemism for universal dislike
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Kent-ism using polling jargon to soften devastating news

jokes.text: You have sharp shoulders. This is like high school all over again. Yeah, sure, kind of, but much bigger.
jokes.punchline: Kent's matter-of-fact escalation to national scale
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kent response that makes everything worse with honesty

jokes.text: Customary shortcuts to public affirmation are military service or childbirth. Okay, God, no, and, oh, my God, no, in that order.
jokes.setup: Kent suggesting ways to improve image
jokes.punchline: Catherine's horrified rejection of both options
jokes.explanation: Great contrast between Kent's clinical approach and Catherine's panic

jokes.text: Okay, well, then we go back to the idea of turning that frown into the inverse of a frown. Upside down?
jokes.setup: Trying to say 'smile' in technical terms
jokes.punchline: Catherine's confused literal interpretation
jokes.explanation: Good Kent moment showing his inability to communicate simply

jokes.text: There's not gonna be a signal, Jonah.
jokes.setup: Jonah asking about a signal for help
jokes.punchline: Dan's blunt admission he won't help
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dan cruelty delivered with efficiency

jokes.text: Hey, what's shakin', bacon? That's my way of saying hello, breaking the ice.
jokes.setup: Trying to be casual with Congressman
jokes.punchline: Over-explaining terrible greeting
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jonah cringe - trying too hard and explaining the obvious

jokes.text: You know, don't think of them as kids as much as little human start-ups.
jokes.punchline: Reducing children to business terminology
jokes.explanation: Brilliant satirical line about viewing children through business lens

jokes.text: And then you take the money you save and you spend it. That's economics.
jokes.punchline: Circular logic presented as profound insight
jokes.explanation: Great satire of political nonsense delivered with false confidence

jokes.text: Did you know that a lot of these have expired?
jokes.punchline: Jonah pointing out the expired coupons
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jonah moment - socially clueless observation that ruins the moment

jokes.text: I'm flying solo. I'm fucking Amelia Earhart and I'm still doing a better job than you.
jokes.setup: Comparing his situation to Dan's team support
jokes.punchline: Comparing himself to doomed aviator while claiming success
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Jonah line - historically inappropriate comparison delivered with total confidence

jokes.text: Now get me some scalps. Names. Get me some names.
jokes.setup: Looking for Native American staff members
jokes.punchline: Using 'scalps' metaphor given the context
jokes.explanation: Borderline offensive wordplay that feels more crude than clever

jokes.text: Yes, it's ironic that you're talking to us about occupation while you occupy someone else's continent.
jokes.punchline: Turning tables on American hypocrisy
jokes.explanation: Sharp political comeback that exposes American hypocrisy

jokes.text: So why don't you tell Sacheen Littlefeather to get off the rag and get over it, all right?
jokes.setup: Frustrated with Native American painting controversy
jokes.punchline: Crude dismissal using racist and sexist language
jokes.explanation: Reveals character's casual bigotry but relies on shock over craft

jokes.text: This was just a preemptive cleaning, much like a juice cleanse.
jokes.punchline: Awkward comparison to trendy health fad
jokes.explanation: Good Mike moment showing his tone-deaf attempts at spin

jokes.text: Cleansed is not the right word. Wiped. No, not wiped. Cleaned.
jokes.punchline: Making it worse with each word choice
jokes.explanation: Good cringe escalation as Mike digs himself deeper

jokes.text: Gary, you look whiter than a Georgia country club.
jokes.punchline: Specific, loaded comparison to exclusive venue
jokes.explanation: Sharp visual description with subtle social commentary

jokes.text: We don't have time for the whole tapestry.
jokes.setup: Gary about to explain his problems
jokes.punchline: Describing his issues as an elaborate artwork
jokes.explanation: Perfect Sue line - efficient metaphor that captures Gary's complexity

jokes.text: Is there a child missing?
jokes.setup: Gary saying he did something 'really bad'
jokes.punchline: Establishing scale of potential badness
jokes.explanation: Good Sue moment establishing baseline for crisis management

jokes.text: Gary, your inner child needs to grow an outer man.
jokes.punchline: Inversion of therapy-speak about inner child
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Sue line - perfect inversion of pop psychology

jokes.text: Boy, at peace with Israel, at war with Native Americans. Do you know what I am? I'm like the opposite of Mel Gibson.
jokes.punchline: Comparison to notoriously anti-Semitic actor
jokes.explanation: Brilliant observation connecting political situation to pop culture scandal

jokes.text: Because the first butt-ugly one was removed by some jerkoff with a hair ball for a brain.
jokes.setup: Need for more Native American paintings
jokes.punchline: Describing Gary in the most insulting terms possible
jokes.explanation: Typical Selina insult - creative but relies on volume over precision

jokes.text: You got all your Danettes and your Dangelinas.
jokes.punchline: Feminizing Dan's staff with pop culture references
jokes.explanation: Creative Jonah insult showing his pop culture-addled brain

jokes.text: What Scorsese film? I don't know. He's got to have a new one dropping, right? He's really prolific.
jokes.setup: Dan questioning Jonah about the made-up screening
jokes.punchline: Jonah doubling down with vague assumptions about Scorsese's output
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Jonah digging himself deeper with confident ignorance

jokes.text: You're flaring your nostrils. It feels natural.
jokes.punchline: Her defensive response to criticism
jokes.explanation: Mild physical comedy showing Catherine's obliviousness

jokes.text: That looked happy, even if you're not happy. That's the trick.
jokes.punchline: Advice about performing happiness
jokes.explanation: Good observation about political performance vs. reality

jokes.text: A lot of volume because her skull is low. It's kind of indented.
jokes.punchline: Brutal assessment of her head shape
jokes.explanation: Mean-spirited physical observation that feels more cruel than funny

jokes.text: And if you stand on it just like so, you can get Wi-Fi.
jokes.setup: Offering Amy a chair as her 'office'
jokes.punchline: Pathetic accommodation requires standing on furniture
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag about workplace dysfunction

jokes.text: I feel like this is gonna be a key scene in my biopic.
jokes.punchline: Grandiose interpretation of mundane moment
jokes.explanation: Good meta joke about self-importance and narrative delusion

jokes.text: Hey, sugar tits, did you stroke and choke the rules committee?
jokes.punchline: Sexual metaphor for political manipulation
jokes.explanation: Crude humor that reveals character but relies on shock value

jokes.text: He's eager and hardworking and never complains and every night I dream of drowning him.
jokes.punchline: Dark confession of murderous thoughts
jokes.explanation: Perfect Amy escalation - building up then devastating with dark humor

jokes.text: I am so proud of you, I could squeeze you like a sponge in the shower.
jokes.punchline: Weirdly specific and somewhat sexual metaphor
jokes.explanation: Creepy Teddy moment with oddly specific physical metaphor

jokes.text: Does that mean, like, he's the epitome of an assistant or he's just an assistant's assistant?
jokes.punchline: Confusion about hierarchy vs. excellence
jokes.explanation: Good Jonah confusion about corporate hierarchies

jokes.text: Am I being 'Punk'd'? Is this hazing? Happened to my brother once. He did not handle it well.
jokes.punchline: Paranoid interpretation and family trauma reference
jokes.explanation: Decent character moment showing Richard's anxiety and oversharing

jokes.text: What's Gary doing? Trying to max out America?
jokes.punchline: Treating the country like a credit card
jokes.explanation: Sharp economic metaphor for governmental overspending

jokes.text: Wow, who knew they made lampshades out of unobtanium?
jokes.punchline: Science fiction reference to fictional expensive material
jokes.explanation: Good nerd reference joke about excessive spending

jokes.text: Yeah, said the Princeton grad in the Valentino tux.
jokes.punchline: Pointing out the speaker's own expensive choices
jokes.explanation: Good hypocrisy callout with specific class markers

jokes.text: That her bagman spends like a Babylonian king?
jokes.punchline: Ancient historical comparison for extravagance
jokes.explanation: Good historical reference for excessive spending

jokes.text: You are in the J-Corps now and we own these hallways.
jokes.punchline: Military-style unit named after himself
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jonah grandiosity - creating military unit for himself

jokes.text: The president and I, we're like this. You know, not to scale, obviously.
jokes.punchline: Acknowledging his height makes gesture meaningless
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Jonah moment - self-aware about his height ruining his gesture

jokes.text: Better not leak any photos. I see your media likes to make a big fuss about how much you spend. Oh, yeah. Yes. Yes. Huh? What? What fuss? What spending?
jokes.setup: Israeli PM commenting on the expensive dinner
jokes.punchline: Selina's panic as she realizes the spending is public knowledge
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Selina's dawning horror at the political implications

jokes.text: Well, imagine Elton John on a day he feels fat.
jokes.setup: Asked about how much Gary spent
jokes.punchline: Specific celebrity comparison for excessive spending
jokes.explanation: Brilliant metaphor combining extravagance with emotional spending

jokes.text: To paraphrase a predecessor of mine, we have nothing to trust but trust itself.
jokes.punchline: Mangling FDR's famous 'nothing to fear but fear itself'
jokes.explanation: Good political speech fumble showing Selina's attempts at gravitas

jokes.text: Who do you think you are, Gary Antoinette? Did somebody make you first lady? 'Cause I don't remember marrying you, Gary. I don't remember fucking you in Niagara Falls.
jokes.punchline: Increasingly personal and sexual insults
jokes.explanation: Classic Selina eruption - escalating from clever wordplay to crude imagery

jokes.text: And you have suckered onto me like some sort of a car window Garfield.
jokes.punchline: Comparing him to cartoon cat car decoration
jokes.explanation: Brilliant visual metaphor - specific, degrading, and perfectly apt

jokes.text: You're a middle-aged man who sanitizes my tweezers.
jokes.punchline: Reducing his role to most menial task possible
jokes.explanation: Perfect Selina putdown - specific, demeaning, and accurate

jokes.text: When's Catherine's birthday? June eighth. Ninth. Which senator's daughter is in rehab?
jokes.setup: Gary proving his value through knowledge
jokes.punchline: Selina immediately getting basic fact wrong
jokes.explanation: Perfect reversal - Gary proving his point while catching Selina in ignorance

jokes.text: I'm your calendar, I'm your Google, I'm your Wilson the volleyball.
jokes.punchline: Comparing himself to inanimate objects, including movie prop
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation ending with perfect 'Cast Away' reference for dependency

jokes.text: Most of the time you don't even know that I exist, but I am fucking everything to you!
jokes.punchline: Contradictory statement about being ignored yet essential
jokes.explanation: Powerful Gary moment - contradictory but emotionally true about toxic relationships

jokes.text: You mean on Labor Day? I didn't say that. Yeah, you did. You just did. You j... you just said Labor Day.
jokes.punchline: Selina's failed attempt to deny acknowledging it
jokes.explanation: Intriguing mystery setup with good character fumbling

jokes.text: It's light sponge. You just told me that.
jokes.punchline: Selina's irritated response to repetition
jokes.explanation: Awkward post-fight moment showing lingering tension

jokes.text: I particularly love this color here. I think this is so pretty, don't you? I believe the title is 'Massacre.'
jokes.punchline: Revelation of violent subject matter
jokes.explanation: Good ironic juxtaposition between aesthetic appreciation and violent content

jokes.text: My, you are small. I suppose you're young, but you're still very small.
jokes.punchline: Awkward focus on child's size
jokes.explanation: Good Catherine awkwardness - inappropriate focus on physical characteristics

jokes.text: Honey, don't look so worried. I'm not gonna bite you. But that man over there does have a gun.
jokes.punchline: Immediately mentioning armed security
jokes.explanation: Perfect Catherine misdirection - trying to comfort but making things worse

jokes.text: Have you ever seen 'Star Wars'? This is a little bit like the end of 'Star Wars.'
jokes.punchline: Comparing to movie award ceremony
jokes.explanation: Decent pop culture reference showing Catherine's disconnect

jokes.text: South Korea has called. They'd like to know if you'd like a baby elephant. I have no response to that. Other than I already have one.
jokes.punchline: Comparing Catherine to baby elephant
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Selina moment - absurd setup leading to cruel daughter comparison

jokes.text: I'm kidding. She used to be chubby when she was little, but she's slimmed down as she's gotten older. Thank goodness.
jokes.punchline: Clarifying with equally awful body commentary
jokes.explanation: Perfect Selina follow-up - making the insult worse while claiming to clarify
